name: Bachelor of Library and Information Science
description:
The Bachelor of Library and Information Science (BLIS) Program is the study of the development, deployment, and management of information sources (print, non-print, and digital formats) and services. The program prepares students to apply Information Technology (IT) in library operations. Students are trained to utilize bibliographical and online tools to support teaching and research.

 Students are also trained to systematically organize, conserve, preserve, and restore information objects (e.g. realia, museum pieces), historical and cultural documents (e.g. artifacts, archival documents, indigenous knowledge), and other intellectual properties (creative ideas and works).
objectives: The Bachelor of Library and Information Science (BLIS) Program aims to prepare students for their unique role in gathering, organizing, curating, managing, and providing access to the best information sources for the knowledge-based organization they belong to, as well as implementing standards for the ethical and appropriate use of information.

name: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science
description:
The BS Computer Science program includes the study of computing concepts and theories, algorithmic foundations and the new developments in computing. The program prepares students to design and create complex software and develop new and effective algorithms for solving computing problems.

 The program also includes the study of the standards and practices in Software Engineering. It prepares students to acquire skills and disciplines required for designing, writing and modifying software components , modules and applications that comprise software solutions.
objectives: The BSCS graduates are expected to become globally competent, innovative and socially and ethnically responsible computing professionals engaged in life-learning endeavors. They are capable of contributing to the country's national development goals.

name: Bachelor of Science in Entertainment and Media Computing
description: Entertainment and Multimedia computing is the study and use of concepts, principles, and techniques of computing in the design and development of multimedia products and solutions. It includes various applications such as in science, entertainment, education, simulations and advertising.
objectives:
The BSEMC program is designed to prepare students to become professionals in the fields of Game Development and Digital Animation Technology. Specifically, the Game Development track equips students with specialized knowledge, competencies, and values for designing, developing, and producing digital games and tools, as well as managing development projects.

 Complementing this, the Digital Animation Technology component ensures students are armed with the creative and technical skills necessary to conceptualize, design, and produce animation solutions, enabling them to effectively manage projects across various technology platforms.

name: Bachelor of Science in Information Systems
description:
The Bachelor of Science in Information Systems (BSIS) program includes the study of comprehensive information systems concepts and the analysis, design, and implementation of IT solutions. The program prepares students with the knowledge, skills, and ethical values necessary to manage information technology in diverse organizational contexts.

 It aims to produce professionals capable of pursuing successful and socially responsible careers by equipping them to analyze, design, and implement technology solutions that address organizational needs.
objectives: Provide students with comprehensive Information systems education, equipping them with the knowledge, skills, and ethical values necessary to analyze, design, implement, and manage information technology solutions in diverse organizational contexts, preparing them for successful and socially responsible careers in the field of Information Systems.

name: Bachelor of Science in Information Technology
description:
The BSIT program aims to equip students with knowledge, skills and attitude in utilizing computer and computer software to plan, install, customize, operate, manage, administer and maintain information technology infrastructure. It also prepares the students to be IT professionals, be well versed on application installation and maintenance.

 BSIT students acquire the following competencies: (a) personal skills, (b) interpersonal skills, and (C) technical understanding on systems analysis and design; operation of databases, networks and multimedia systems; software integration, testing and documentation; and systems management and administration.
objectives:
The program aims to produce competent graduates with sufficient exposure to the real-world environment through on-the-job training and educational trips. It prepares students with a broad liberal education in the arts, communications, social sciences, and philosophy , while equipping them with broad and specific research capabilities applicable to their chosen area of specialization.

 Furthermore, the curriculum develops an understanding of professional, ethical, legal, security, and social responsibilities. Ultimately, the program engages students in research, extension, and development, enabling them to innovate, invent, and develop ICT processes and products that help elevate human lives.

name: Master in Information Technology
description: The Master in Information Technology (MIT) program gives students the opportunity to deepen their IT knowledge and allow them to study new and emerging areas of advanced information technology (IT). This program provides a comprehensive postgraduate education in information technology directed at IT educators, IT professionals and recent graduates seeking to update their knowledge.
objectives:
Graduates are expected to apply advanced knowledge of information technology principles and practices to evaluate existing IT solutions, as well as to innovate or create new ones tailored to organizational needs. They are capable of identifying, planning, analyzing, designing, and implementing solutions to address complex IT-related problems , utilizing research and correct systems analysis principles to develop stable, secure, scalable, and maintainable IT products.

 Additionally, they recommend appropriate solutions while assuring system confidentiality, integrity, and availability , and manage the use and maintenance of these solutions according to globally accepted standards and practices. Beyond technical application, they utilize their advanced knowledge to provide expertise in both industry and academic settings, mentoring IT professionals for continued career growth and life-long learning.
